hotel_name: Sofitel Bali Nusa Dua Beach Resort

reviews.title: 2026 visit - horrible, avoid
reviews.text: This hotel is luxury apparently but it’s literally beaten down. The pool is literally broken, tiles everywhere. The bathrooms are beaten down, it feels grimy. Especially visiting with a baby I was paranoid the whole time. The other thing is staff, pool staff were not attentive at all, the breakfast buffet I sat down for like 10 mins while my husband changes our baby; the staff literally harassed me by asking questions as if I’m not even a guest or haven’t paid for my breakfast(mind you I wasn’t even eating, I was just sitting and it  wasn’t busy either) I would say avoid this hotel at all COST. The staff seems stingy to me. I shouldn’t believe the recent reviews.
reviews.response:
Bonjour, Manaal!

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. It is sad to know that your overall stay was not fully in line with your expectations.

At Sofitel Bali, our team strives to deliver attentive and thoughtful service to every guest. This commitment is often reflected in the positive recognition we receive regarding their dedication and professionalism.

Regarding your experience in the breakfast area, please be assured that our team had no negative intention while you were waiting. Their approach forms part of ensuring that each guest is properly attended to and that service is delivered accurately and seamlessly. It is unfortunate that this interaction was perceived differently and resulted in an uncomfortable impression.

Your comments are noted with care, as we remain committed to delivering a refined and thoughtful experience throughout the resort. We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you again and provide a stay that is even more seamless and truly enjoy

reviews.title: We will not be returning to this resort.
reviews.positives: This is not a 5 star experience. The common areas are nice and the location is great on the beach, but the rooms we stayed at were in need of an urgent upgrade and deeper cleaning. We were celebrating a birthday and were surprised by a nice small treat which was a nice gesture and appreciated.
reviews.negatives:
We booked two rooms with plunge pools. Interior finishes are dated and in need of an upgrade. The fabric wall panels had dust on them, we found nail clippings on the floor, mold in the shower in one room and mold on the wall in the other, stains on the couch. I reported it to a lady at the reception desk. She apologized and said she would report to management and would request a deep clean. Someone did show up and tried cleaning, but the mold stains were still there and the floor still felt dirty, couch was still stained.
Beach chairs are very dated and need of a deeper cleaning or to be replaced.
We ate at the buffet and were not impressed with the food choices and felt that it was overpriced. We  ordered food at Toya twice and both times waited for way too long for our food and drinks to be served, 35+ min wait. We were offered a complimentary juice because it was not served until after we had finished our food. Both times it was just us and another table, so it’s not like they were too busy and couldn’t catch up with orders. 
Food at the Cucina restaurant was good and service was the best we experienced at this resort. We felt it was overpriced though.

reviews.title: Very bad service, the staff are so rude
reviews.text: We had our honey moon and I regret that I choose Sofitel, the bed smells so bad and we asked the receptionist many times to change it but they didn’t. Prices are over high in all the restaurants and the spa is also so expensive.
reviews.response:
Bonjour, Ihab!

Thank you for sharing your feedback following your recent stay at our resort. I regret to understand that your special occasion spent at our hotel was not entirely up to your expectations. After proper investigation, we believe that you were requesting us to refresh the room, and not for a room move. It is very unfortunate that this feedback was not made clear during your stay and I will have this further reviewed with the relevant team. Thank you for sharing this with us and we hope to better serve you in the future.

Regards,
Anne
Quality & Guest Experience Manager

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives:
I am writing to express my extreme dissatisfaction with my recent stay at Sofitel Nusa Dua. I had high expectations for this property, but unfortunately, my experience was well below standard for a hotel of your reputation.
	1.	Non-functional Phone Lines: The phone line in my room either did not work or was always busy, making it nearly impossible to reach hotel staff when needed. This caused significant inconvenience during my stay.
	2.	Unreliable Wi-Fi: The hotel’s Wi-Fi was highly unreliable, working only about half the time. For a luxury hotel, consistent internet connectivity should be a basic standard, and this was simply unacceptable.
	3.	Early Closures of Facilities: The fact that all hotel facilities closed by 10 PM left guests with nowhere to go, especially during the frequent rainy evenings. For a resort in a tropical destination, this lack of flexibility is disappointing.
	4.	Rigid Check-In and Check-Out Policies: Despite not allowing us to check in until 3 PM, the hotel refused to grant a late check-out past 11 AM. This lack of accommodation was frustrating and did not make guests feel valued.
	5.	Poor Room Service Options: The room service menu was extremely limited, and the food quality was subpar. None of the options were remotely satisfying, which is not what one expects from a luxury hotel.

Overall, my stay at Sofitel Nusa Dua was disappointing, and I feel the hotel failed to provide the level of service and quality advertised. I would never recommend this hotel for anyone travelling to Bali.

reviews.title: Bad experience
reviews.text:
During our stay, our room has a pungent smell coming from the renovation upstairs (2nd floor). And there was loud banging on occasions during the night. 

We booked king size bed, but was given 2 single beds instead.
reviews.room_type: LUXURY ROOM, 1 King Size Bed, Plunge Pool

reviews.title: Awfully disappointing stay.  Poor recreational facilities beyond the pool and gym.
reviews.positives: Hotel is very nicely laid out with lots of greenery.  Staff were very polite and helpful (other than the reception staff who made a mess of our room assignment at check-in).  The room was spacious but the fridge door could not close tightly and it leaked water all over the floor.  It had to get it repaired. The aircon kept stopping after a while and we had get it reset by the maintenance. The all-day restaurant was large but hot and mosquito-infested.  The seafood barbecue at Toya restaurant was good, but they should have appliances to deal with the large number of flies and mosquitos which ruined the whole experience.  Compliments though to the pastry chef of Kwee Zeen.  The breads and croissants were excellent.  On the whole, for this pricing, there should be much better options than this hotel.
reviews.negatives: Hotel is in an area that is secured by a security guard.  Nothing around except the beach and the sea.  You are captive to the exhorbitant pricing of the restaurants within these confines. While they have an hourly shuttle bus to a group of shops and restaurants outside the secured area, the choices there are also pretty limited.  We booked a room that was advertised as one with an "extra large king bed", resort view and "pool view".  We could see the resort from our room but the rest were gross exaggerations.  The bed was normal size - certainly not "extra-large".  The so-called pool view required us to arch our necks over the balcony to see this sliver of water that was more like a drain from the main pool which was nowhere in sight.  To add insult to injury, when we checked-in we were assigned a twin-bed room, when our reservation was expressly for a double room with their self-touted "extra-large king bed".  The welcome note on the table was even addressed to someone else.  We had to wait in this room until another room was made up and ready for us.  Food-wise, it was a wretched disappointment.  We had our dinner at the Cucina Italian restaurant.  It was not cheap and yet, the tagliatelle pasta was served with the strands all stuck together.  We then tried the Kwee Zeen all-day dining restaurant.  The food was more acceptable for its price-point but it was hot with no aircon and a few ineffective fans.  The buffet breakfast was horribly way below the standard of a high-end hotel.  The buffet spread was wide enough but some of the dishes were cooked as if just to make up the numbers.  A fried seafood noodle, had no visible seafood.  The noodles and sausages at the noodle station were rancid. There was no games room. Use of a carrom board had to be booked in advance to set-up (basically, sitting on the floor in a hot pavillion).

reviews.title: so bad.
reviews.text:
I booked this resort this holiday.
Room condition not suitable for 5 stars
There was dust in the room and a spider web in the bathroom.
The exterior is gorgeous, but the interior is old.

I stayed in a full access room, but said they would upgrade to a suite, but I didn't want it. 
I booked this room for the children.

And there were too many mosquitoes at night as it was close to the pool.
This is the area to be concerned about.
I use several hotels in Agoda, but this is the first time I have been disappointed and terrible.

The staff responded well, but this is a hotel with a lot of things to fix.

This hotel is not recommended for family travel.
There are many other 5star hotels in Nusa Dua, so it is a good idea to choose another one.

reviews.title: Lacking in quality
reviews.text: The rooms were in a state of disrepair. This for a USD$300/night room. We opt for a room with attached outdoor private plunge pool. 1st room we were given had a leaking toilet when it flushed, then after a shower , water would leak into the room itself. the walls of the toilet were dirty, the floor in the shower stall had a lot of white residue that simply looks unpleasant. The shower handle mount was damaged and the room smelled so musky. Worse when you turn on the water. We had a tour booked and so we informed the front desk and was told that they will move us to another room. Well back , some improvements in that the shower mount not being damaged and smelled less musky but the outdoor area hanging chair was with stain marks and I can go on. As it was our 15th wedding anniversary, this was an even more painful experience. The front desk did however leave a bottle of wine as a gesture but a small token in what would be an important vacation / anniversary for my wife and I.
reviews.positives: Landscape and layout good but upkeep of property is severly lacking

reviews.title: Sub-standard service & false advertising
reviews.text: I wouldn't stay there again. The 'upgrade-d' room was not an upgrade but downgrade and I couldn't sleep all night due to noise outside. I had to call front office numerous times to have room changed. We were trapped indoors all Sat. Front desk suggested we book breakfast with them - upon check out I informed them I did not go to breakfast and they insisted it was a 'package' so no refund - given I had yet to have even paid for it. I reminded them that they had not mentioned anything about a package but they charged me anyways. The bathroom smelt like rotten eggs or a sewer - every time you shower, it stank. Welcome to a 5 star resort. Obviously you don't get what you pay for.

reviews.title: Not up to the regular Sofitel standards
reviews.text:
Do not have high expectations - overpriced considering the service received. Our room was not cleaned properly which was reported and although effort was put into cleaning the room this was definitely not up to the usual Sofitel standard. We had a problem with the the fact that a lot of the wait staff did not fully comprehend english and often misunderstood our requests.

One of the most disappointing things was that it took four days for management to actually involve themselves with the daily mishaps and this was after us having to continuously follow up.

Attention to detail is definitely lacking. We booked over Christmas and New Years and was forced to pay double the room charge for the dinner on 31 December 2017. At approximately $310 for both my husband and I the dinner was most disappointing!!! An ordinary buffet with very poor service. BE WARY if you book during this time rather query the compulsory dinner charge - you will be disappointed. Rather opt out and go elsewhere.

I had to wait 1.5 hours to check into my room. Initially the staff said 30 minutes and then 1 hour later we asked and he said another 30 minutes. When we went into the room, the phone was not working properly. The bathtub looked dirty (very different from picture). The minibar was leaking and the wood was rotting at the bottom of the minibar, such that on the 3rd day there were mushrooms sprouting, (yes i took pictures to show the manager who was equally shocked).

Overall a disappointing experience for the price I paid. And yes i complained and got 1 free meal, that's all.. They could have upgraded or changed my room, or given me some discount/money refund, but did not happen. 

Highly recommend you choose another resort, for that price you can get much better elsewhere.

reviews.title: Just Okay
reviews.text:
We have stayed at Sofitel Nusa Dua five times since 2018, and while we initially loved this resort, the standard of service has sadly declined with each visit. Regrettably, this recent stay will likely be our last.

The rooms remain beautifully designed and comfortable, showcasing the luxury you’d expect from a Sofitel property. However, small but impactful oversights, like a missing chair at our dining table, made simple activities like having a meal with my partner unnecessarily awkward. We also had problems with hot water and the lock on the back door to our plunge pool room was broken making it possible for anyone to walk into our room.

The most noticeable issue, however, is with the food and beverage service. It’s apparent that a large portion of the staff consists of daily workers, and unfortunately, this has led to a lack of organization. During the Christmas brunch, for example, we waited 45 minutes for drinks, and 30 minutes for a coffee the following morning at breakfast. the table service felt rushed and impersonal—hardly what you’d expect from a 5-star resort. Also sad to see chipped and cracked plates at the buffet.

Sofitel Nusa Dua still has a lot of potential, but the service standards and attention to detail need significant improvement to justify themselves as a 5 star resort. For now, we’ll be looking elsewhere for our next holiday.

Anne

reviews.negatives:
If you like being on vacation with other people, and not feeling like you can experience culture- then this place is for you! The rooms are ransacked and are not luxurious at all. The rooms are teeny tiny and way too expensive in my opinion for what you can get in Bali. If you go 20 mins down the road, you can get an entire villa with a private pool for a fraction of the price. 
When resorts like this try to accommodate the western cuisine, instead of just sticking to their local cuisine-- you really don't get great food. Sadly, I don't even think they offered nasi goreng (a national staple) which is WILDDDD
I didn't realize Nusa Dua is completely gated and guarded from all locals. Its purely resorts. We know the price gauging happens, but its absurd how much steeper it was here. Not our vibe, but could be yours! Bali is such a beautiful place and this resort (or any resort in Nusa Dua) does not capture that!

reviews.title: Inproper Reservation Rule
reviews.text:
3 rooms and 2 nights were booked at this hotel for my guest. Voucher stated payment at Property, but my CC were charge for 3 nights by the HOTEL directly. 

I have called the hotel to find out and they informed that Guest has to present ORIGINAL of my CC at the front desk during check in. I have informed the staff that I will not follow the guest during check in, I even offered to the hotel staff to send me an authorized letter for me to sign so that they will not ask from guest again. This is the first time I met hotel with such a stiff rule. The staff insist and said my CC need to be bring by the guest or otherwise they will charge to the guest directly. My CC will be refunded only if the guest has paid to the hotel during c/o and refund will be released after 2 weeks. Meanwhile during these 2 weeks the charge will be shown in the CC statement which we need to pay as well ( double charge for the 3 rooms) The hotel leave us with no choice, Even the agoda CS called the hotel but the answer is the same. Fortunately, the guest is our company executives whom I can trust to pass my CC to.
reviews.response:

As you mentioned, we had alerted you beforehand of this practice and you could manage to find one of the many solutions in time before your guests checked-in.

Overall this hotel is nice except for the bed and pillow. Imagine if you ever go into duck farm. Well its exactly the same smell when you sleep on that bed but inside sofitel. The smell of the duck pillow and everything was too strong and unpleasant, uncomfortable
reviews.positives: Clean, new hotel and big moderrn room

reviews.title: Experience the unexpected
reviews.text: We only stayed for 1 night (23 Dec) because great promo rate during Sofitel grand opening on 20 Dec. We checked in at around 3.30 pm and was surprised because there were some mosquitos inside our room. We first thought it might because we took Luxury Room with direct pool access however then found out that mosquitos could be easily found out in evey areas of the hotel. Called the receptin however was informed that no other luxury room was available due to full occupation. Then we took dinner buffet and again were surprised with the only few varieties of the food. I recommend that others should check the food counters first before deciding to have the buffet.
reviews.positives: Nice design, direct pool access and high speed free wifi access

reviews.title: This is our cond visit and we got disappointed...
reviews.text:
Our first visit (10 months ago) was excellent, so we went back. Now we're not sure if we'll go back again.

Ladies toilet in the lobby, with smart system like ones in Japan, the remotes were not working. Other than that, lobby area were great. Cleaners sweeping every seconds, erasing footprints on (I wonder why) matte black floor.

One of the natural stone tiles on top of our plunge pool terrace (#6015) fell off, lucky not on anyone's head! After swimming, our hot water shower was not working. They made us check in at 3pm, and the room is still not in prime condition. When the rain shower is finally fixed, 20% of the nossles didn't spray. Shampoo, conditioner, and soap were unfortunately lower smoothness quality and different brand with the last time we visit.

Swimming from my room to main pool, there were 2 cans (coke and beer) floating on the pool, for 2 days. Some of the ga in front of plunge pool rooms are dry, plants are dying or dead, we noticed at least one electricity cable proturlde from the soil which was dangerous. Such a pity for a wonderful hotel 10 months ago that we miss a lot.

Sofitel Nusa Dua 30 minutes signature fire dance which was dreamy fantastic with cool music vibe on every evening, now has no music *sob* it was almost like watching a strange firey Charlie Chaplin movie..

Sofitel club flowing food and wine is still high quality and rich in detail, balancing international and local concepts beautifully. Breakfast buffet still have great selection of culinary indulgence, although service was quite slow picking up our plates. And it was not their busy day, there was no line... trust me we know their busy day well when we must wait 15 minutes to get a table for breakfast. We miss the HongKong cooks in Asian section though, simply for show. But current cooks also did good job making yummy food.

I miss Sofitel Nusa Dua. The price is never cheap, we know.. but the value WAS delivered nicely. Please bring back your attention on detail.
reviews.positives: Plunge Pool with Room Access. White sand beach right behind the hotel.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
To be fair, the resort is beautiful and the facilities are plenty. 

HOWEVER, service is nowhere near 5 star standard. Service does not anticipate your needs, and with the constant run ins with housekeeping (almost everything I needed the room, they were in it. And one of the housekeeping staff did not leave the bathroom despite me rushing in to use the WC (obscured view but still)), unkempt dining facilities (missing glass from the breakfast table, seat with debris, and wet chairs - understandable once in a while but it was repeatedly happening everyday) and very mediocre food, it was disappointing. 

An incident that really ruined Sofitel Bali for me is: I am currently pregnant and was having dinner at Cucina (in the hotel) and I was served a complimentary drink without being told that it was alcoholic. As being unable to consume alcohol right now, I of course wouldn’t have dipped it if I had known it was a cocktail. I only realised when  a supervisor exclaimed when he saw me drinking it. Needless to say I was furious and extremely upset and complained to who I think is the restaurant manager. Because what kind of policy is it to not tell guests what they are consuming?! The restaurant manager actually said these words to me ‘I cannot say anything right now’. No service recovery was offered. And despite lodging a formal complaint with the duty manger, I was still charged the full price of the meal (which I had to force myself to expunge to get rid of the alcohol)  upon check out. 

I was given late check out as some sort of service recovery (I had to go to the duty manager myself because the restaurant did not escalate but were tactless and sprouting useless apologies with no action) but I cannot believe on the basis of principle that they still charged me for the meal that endangered my health. 

Horrible experience that has ruined my holiday and I will never recommend or return Sofitel Bali ever again.

reviews.title: Very Disappointed
reviews.text: This is our 15th visit to Bali and we have stayed in most of the 5 Star hotels and would have to say this was the most disappointing. We found that that the staff  in many cases seemed like they were training there English was poor, we waited a long time for food and drinks and on many occasions what we ordered was not delivered. The hotel notified us that they were having a 3 day wedding but failed to tell us that the wedding would take priority over most of the resort which also involved a DJ with loud doof doof music blasting most of the day which certainly cleared most of the guests that were relaxing by the pool prior to the 5 hour party. The guests then partied into the night on both the Friday and Saturday night yelling out in the hallways in the early hours of the morning and waking us on several occasions. We were not the only guests unhappy with the situation everyone was complaining by the pool. Needless to say we wont be back or recommending the hotel.

Another time asked for some cold water and it never came until the end of the meal..  so I got it myself from their service counter and ever since I just self-served everything and no staff approached me to offer assistance. Horrible horrible customer service. Very disappointing for a so called 5 stars hotel..

reviews.title: Still not a mature luxury hotel
reviews.text:
I have to be frank that the service of Sofitel needs to be further improved. 
There are few observations that led to my feedback above.

Firstly, check-in itself took us more than half an hour. I was waiting unattended when I first arrived. I can feel that the staff is not checking in the guest efficient enough and also the management needs to overcome under-staff issue as well. When I booked my club room, i was being informed that we will be having private check-in however I do not receive any.
After a long check-in, it then took long time for the welcoming drinks too. 

Secondly, staff does not speak fluent English. Few misunderstand happened when we ask where we could have our club room wine tasting session, we were led by one of the staff to their common restaurant. Later, we find our own way to the club lounge. The club privilege status just dropped the moment we not being treated as a club room guest.

Thirdly, couple of time, we were finding way to other corner of the hotel and were asking around. Not much of the staffs are helpful, not because they are not friendly enough, but we just cannot seem to understand each others. That was disappointing experience for a luxury hotel. More training required for the staff.

Fourth, miscommunication with the staff when we requested for an indoor table during breakfast. The staff keep pointing us to the table at the open area while we can see still many unoccupied table indoor. 

Other feedback include, club lounge is situated too far from the club room. And also the club dinning table need to be more. We are dinning almost like sharing table with others. We don't mind sharing, but what if the guests would like to have some private dinning experience with their partner? Hope the management can take in this feedback openly.

Overall the cleanliness is fine, room spacious, great trademarked pillow, courteous coffee vending machine in-room, awesome bath amenities.
reviews.positives: big room, great pool, sunrise

reviews.title: The fall of a grand luxurious resort
reviews.text:
In the end my stay was very enjoyable, but to get to get that result took a lot of energy from me. 

I'm not sure what has happened to this resort, it used to be AMAZING, but it has aged so poorly. Whoever is running the resort should be sacked immediately, literally SACK THE TOP MANAGER FOR BEING BLIND (not the local manager, I mean the top suit european executive), the place needs a good CLEAN from top to bottom. Seriously, it is an amazing place but PLEASE CLEAN IT... and Sofitel, stop being so cheap and spend some money on upgrades!!! You can't be that blind, just ignorant. You are letting this magical luxurious place fall to pieces. Having stayed here in 2015, 2016, 2017 it was truly luxurious (hence my return in 2023), and I understand resorts age but this one has taken a terrible tumble. 

I could go on with a massive list.... however the staff went over & above to help me and literally resolve all my issues beyond my expecations, so in the end I thoroughly enjoyed my stay. BUT.... Sofitel Management - PICK UP YOUR ACT! Stop letting this place fall apart. The Sofitel name conveys luxury and quality. 

From talking to the staff I think COVID has hit this place very very badly, but that's no excuse for a deep pocketed company like Sofitel. Spend money and FIX IT AND FOR GOD'S SAKE CLEAN IT! Furthermore, train your staff to speak better english, I know there's a staff shortage but c'mon, respect them and train them or the progression. They give you everything and you give them nothing. 

But Thank You for making my stay enjoyable, I doubt i'll be back until you renovate, HOWEVER I WOULD recommend it the more common basic people because it is 5-star luxury (if they cleaned it, modernised it, upgraded it). If I was after an "affordable" 5-star holiday then I would stay again, I'd just take bleach lol. 

If you are happy flying economy and maybe business, then yes you will LOVE this place, but if you fly First Class... then you may need to adjust your expecatations
reviews.response:

Bonjour! Selamat Siang!

Thank you for choosing us again, following the recent visit you had in the Island of Paradise. 

It fills us with remorse knowing that the latest stay you had was not entirely up to your expectations. Please rest assured as I will discuss this further with the relevant team for further review and immediate actions.

At Sofitel, we always value our guest's feedback as we believe it could help us much to improve the quality of products and services in the future, hence, we would like to thank you once again for sharing it with us. 

Nonetheless, I hope you will give us another chance to serve you better in the future.

Agus Astawa

reviews.text:
It's a five star hotel - so you expect everything is excellent. 

We had to wait for about almost an hour to get into our room. Check in time was at 3pm. We arrived there just before 3. So we only get into our room at 5 mins before 4. I travelled a lot and never in my experience that I have to wait this long ... and it was way after the check in time! that's mad! We were asked to enjoy the welcome drink. After a long wait I had to go back to check with the reception if the room is ready. So imagine if I did not go back to them. When we get into our room - the room smells really really bad.  but I was wondering maybe it's because the room was empty for so long as a result of COVID-19 no customers etc. Thus, I did not bother to compain. Also already tired after a long trip ... and it's already 4. What to expect they moved us to other room? that only delayed our time to rest. Staff at the Restaurant are amazing. But not their front desk. We checked in for 4 nights. on our third night we arrived at 8pm. We cant open the room. After asking some help - including calling the reception from the phone on the corridor - they asked me for my booking number - etc after I provided my room number. Apparently that's not enough!!! I had then to walk to the front desk to get my card key fixed. We will not going back to Sofitel.

reviews.title: Sofitel nusa dua bali
reviews.text: Booked a luxury suite with private plunge pool for 2 nights.  Check in time at 3pm but we only got the room at 3.40pm. Waited at the lobby lounge for 30 mins before being escorted to club millesime for high tea while we wait for the room to be ready. For guests with club access do note slippers are not allowed in for men. The bathtub shower handle in our room came sticky, which was not very pleasant. One of the tv controller was faulty as well. Breakfast spread was huge but i observed some of the guests queuing to wait for tables as the restaurant was full. The kids had a great time at the kids pool. Spent an afternoon and the beach house which was pretty great. Dinner at cucina was awesome. Most of the staffs are friendly. Overall an okay experience but i would probably think twice again to come back, food and drinks expenses at the resort are on the high side if you are planning to spend majority of the time in the resort.

reviews.title: Breakfast Total Disappointment
reviews.text:
The breakfast area of this hotel is extremely disappointing- it is noisy, chaotic, disfunctional with often poor quality and limited selection of local fruits. Table and staff service virtually non existent. It feels more like a Shopping Mall Food Court than a 4+ star hotel. We discovered many guests, despite have breakfast included, were eating at two of the adjoining hotels despite the additional cost.
Despite also spending 4 nights at this hotel we could not dine in its restaurants as they were fully booked and as such never ate a dinner there. We did however have excellent meals and service for our period of stay at Westin, Laguna and Kempinski and should have stayed at one of these hotels. We booked Sofitel as it was new but our experience has ensured will will never utilise this group again. 
( we are regular travellers in Bali and Asia 
plus Europe and US across 20+ years)

Once again, thank you for sharing your feedback with us and I hope you will give us another opportunity to serve you better in the future. 

Sincerely yours,
Sylvain Pasdeloup
General Manager
Sofitel Bali Nusa Dua Beach Resort

reviews.title: Great location, mostly great service
reviews.positives: The location, room and facilities
reviews.negatives: Service at the F&B venues, especially at Cuchina. One of the worst dinner I’ve had in terms of service.

reviews.title: Family friendly however a lot of dissatisfaction when i stay
reviews.text:
First we emailed the hotel to ask for early check in however they said its upon availability, so we rent a car to go and have lunch at ubud so we come to the hotel nicely around 3.30. notice that the check in time was 3 pm, we arrive around 3.30 pm. HOWEVER our room is not ready! and we wait until around 4.15 and i think it was so unreasonable for that ! I walk back and forth to the check in desk and the answer was just that the room is not ready and we have to wait.. until aroub 4.15 i really so upset and asked for the manager to come and explain.. and finally he went down to the room him self and make sure the room is ready... but still we wait about half an hour for it...  so about 1.5 hour we wait for our room to be ready! 

The room size was just OK, We booked the room with the plunge pool inside. 
the shower place design was so bad that everytime we took a shower, the water run out from the shower place and i have to put a big towel to prevent the water flow out

reviews.positives: I like the location directly to the beach
reviews.negatives: I don't like the front office/duty manager responding to my complaint. I complained by calling the front office at 3am when a Korean guest had just checked in next to my room. But I had to call 5 times until an hour later before the noise was resolved with the excuse that Mao was calling Korean guests, the duty manager and security guard Mao came to the room. My question is how far is the room from the front office? I was very disappointed to only get an apology without thinking about our comfort

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.positives: Good location, staff was friendly.
reviews.negatives: Not completely worth the amount of money paid. Water issues in the room no hot water sometimes and water supply was completely closed at night, amenities were not fully provided in the room initially. The room was stinky and was changed later next day. Overall the furniture was cheap and didn’t give a good clean look like couch, chairs etc in the room. No feedback asked at check out. Overall didn’t feel like a 5 star stay.

reviews.title: Great pools but wouldn't return to Nusa Dua
reviews.text: This  is an enormous sprawling hotel and was a bit of a shock to us after staying at a beautiful boutique hotel in Ubud.  The pools are wonderful  and fantastic fun for adults and children alike. Our room was made up to accommodate both children which we really appreciate & the grounds are nicely landscaped and well kept. We ate at Cucina and the beach cafe ( next to a construction site) and found the service and food quality inadequate at both locations,  particularly given the prices charged.  The breakfasts are fine though, with plenty of choice. We had a suite which came with club lounge access , which would be better if the club lounge itself was a more appealing place to be - it's a little dreary.  The bar in the lobby overlooking grass rooves and pool is a much more appealing destination for an evening drink. Other than in the restaurants  staff were very helpful and friendly and assisted us with restaurant / spa bookings.
reviews.response:

reviews.title: Not again .. Sofitel
reviews.positives: Location and Ambience
reviews.negatives: Staffs were not welcoming at all and seems very transactional. If we are paying or spending on something only then we were greeted else no smile at all.

reviews.title: Just an OK Hotel
reviews.text: We had to wait more than 30mins to get our room.  The room smells bad when we check in because of pest control fogging.  Cleanliness of the room can be improved.  Overall, service level went down from the last time we stayed here a couple of years ago.

reviews.title: Lapses
reviews.text:
Ambience was fantastic.  However, we saw a railing separated at the 'joint' revealing extremely sharp edges.  This would have been dangerous incident  had we used the railing to climb up the pool.  This needs immediate attention.  I think the location was the railing in the pool next to the Jacuzzi…there was no around at 8am on Monday to inform staff of this.

Poor communication regarding our reservation (Agoda-front office - breakfast restaurant).  We were not expected at check in.  After paying for an upgrade to the King access pool - we were told breakfast wasn't included each morning for the 2 days we were there.  One day I can accept but the next day to be presented with a bill??…despite speaking to a 'senior'?

We had 3 different timings from different staff when we asked about the afternoon tea that was offered with the upgrade (which I didn't know it was)…..there was a 'tapas' timing too??
reviews.positives: staff hospitality at the dining outlets (breakfast and CC) and club.

reviews.title: Huge hotel with great view and staff
reviews.text:
Excellent hotel with friendly staff and great facilities. I was there only for one night and hence my stay was short.

The staff from Vincent checking us in and out to the PTs in the gym it brings out the best of Balinese and Indonesian friendly nature. The gym was superb (yes I am a fitness freak) although it was like a maze trying to find it. 

The rooms were spacious and very clean with generous range of toiletries. There are beautiful walkways with a nice clean beach. Very quiet and serene surroundings unlike the hotels near Kuta Beach.

A must stay if you are in Nusa Dua.
reviews.positives: Hotel design, location and staff

reviews.title: Disorganized club service, fail to deliver
reviews.text: We stayed here for only 2 nights and they had to swop us 4 rooms for such a short stay. We waited 2 hrs to check into the first room, we booked a suite room, 88sq M, but was assigned the 49sq M size instead, and the room could not fit the extra bed we had requested for the 3rd guest. Waited 3 hrs later and they assigned a 88 sq M suite but with no view. (When we requested ocean view) poor coordination from the butler. This suite was a great size, but the bathroom had a defect, the water from the shower floods the bathroom as the water flows away from the floor trap, now we had to request another room as it created a big mess on the nice bathroom! So we obviously have the hotel feedback abt it plus insisted that they must give us a room w a view since we paid so much for it. We were transferred to a nice suite w a great ocean view but they fail to keep to their promise of late check out even though we double check several times. 2 hours before out agreed checkout time, the butler called to tell is that we have to check out in 10 mins and they will transfer us to another room!( 4th room in 2 nights) I was really upset! Couldn't believe it! This hotel has nice grounds and well kept! Just the communication w the staff that needs to improve, as they cannot promise what they cannot deliver!
reviews.positives: Nice grounds, friendly staff, nice pool

reviews.title: Improvement
reviews.text: Nice peaceful quiet resort and resort staff especially at the baggage waiting and receptionist areas are very helpful and polite . Keep it up. Except that no new bath towels are replaced the next day at the room ...only face towels are available. The bins in the room are not emptied too. Housekeeping at room is not too good. Maybe need to improve. But overall the resort service is very good .
reviews.positives: Location, Food & Service

reviews.title: Hotel with nice and comfort properties
reviews.text:
It was an enjoyable stay, kids loved the pool and also the beach. Room also nice, with private plunge pool and nice sun bathing sofa. it's suitable for honeymooner or family with young children. But if you have an older children, it's better for you to take room with pool access.
Nice view and also good hotel properties.
reviews.positives: Nice room, Excellent Swimming Pool and Great kids corner

Service at the restaurant also not great, we hate to wait and asking for numerous time before the item requested brought to the table.  Room service surprisingly quick, presentable and professional.

reviews.title: Not the worst, could be better!
reviews.text:
Booked and stayed for 3 nights. Check in process was very slow. We had to wait 1 hr just to get a room and with a one year old at 9pm wasn’t fun. 
We stayed in a King room which was quite spacious and looks exactly like the photos depict, which is a bonus considering how trick photography works. Room amenities are basic but free bottle water was supplied which you’d expect from a 5star resort.
In my opinion Sofitels Achilles heel is its age. I’m sure this would have been an immaculate hotel in the past. Great pool area and having the beach front was special. Breakfast options were plentiful and of quality. Service and smiles everywhere which is Bali! All in all not the top end of 5-Star but not the worst.

reviews.title: Good location good beach
reviews.text: Located in Nusa Dua alongside hotels with private beach, but its quite far from the nightlife in Seminyak or Canggu, suitable for families

reviews.title: ok
reviews.text:
I was staying in the area and decided to try this hotel as I am a frequent traveller to Bali. Firstly the rooms were very nice, well presented, clean had good-sized balconies, basically everything you would expect from a furnishings point of view for $300 a night.

the noise from the neighbour was excessive and went on until late, if this was every night I would have asked to move, also the noise from the corridor was at some times excessive as well

I ate at the pool bar, which was average and the Beach restaurant which was excellent, ironically the beach side restaurant was cheaper and better value, also the service was excellent. Buffet breakfast in the morning was good, though the coffee was well average

The one thing I noticed was there was no drinking water in the room, there was initially 2 bottles of free water that would not of suited 2 people for daily consumption and then there was plenty of water in the mini bar but at $8.5 aus this is extortion. overall - average

reviews.title: Lovely family hotel
reviews.text: We had a lovely three night stay at the Sofitel. Having two small children 2yrs and 4yrs we liked how close the rooms were to the pools and the kids club had a great fenced outdoor space. The pools were the highlight for us. Such a beautiful setting. We enjoyed dining at the resort restaurants and swim up bar. The beach club next door is lovely although the music can be heard in the rooms at night. The buffet breakfast was run really well with quite a good selection. More traditional dishes would have been welcomed in the Asian section. We would love to come back and stay at The Sofitel Nusa Dua.

reviews.title: Lovely, relaxing resort
reviews.text: My wife and I chose Sofitel Nusa Dua as our short honeymoon getaway after our wedding, which was also in Bali. Check-in was disastrous - we checked in at 4.30pm, way after the 3pm check-in time as we did not want to risk having to wait for a room. When we were checking in, we were told that the room was not ready and we would have to wait an hour for the room to be ready, all without apology. In the end, we chose to upgrade as we were tired and did not want to spend an hour waiting in the lobby for our room. It was a disappointing start to our honeymoon and we expected service levels to be higher in a 5-star international chain. To be fair, the housekeeping and Cucina restaurant staff were fantastic. Good variety in their breakfast spread, although doesn't change much day to day. Would recommend Cucina or the pool cafe which shares the same kitchen, but we noticed that it lacks consistency - probably different chefs. Would not recommend in-room dining, it is a different kitchen from Cucina. The food quality for Club Millisime lounge high tea and cocktails need to be improved - the some of the high tea pastries tasted stale. Overall, loved the room and pool, but service levels and food quality could be improved.
reviews.response:
Dear Valued Guest,

Bonjour! Selamat Siang!

We thank you for writing a review on our Agoda page.

We are honoured to have had the chance of accommodating you for your honeymoon. Your commendations for our housekeeping and Cucina restaurant are truly appreciated. However, I regret to read about some inconvenience you have experienced especially about your check in. Please be ensured that I have personally shared this matter with our Front Office Manager and Executive Housekeeper for immediate actions to avoid repeat occurrence in the future.

We thank you once more for your feedback and we look forward to welcoming you back at our resort in not too distant future.

Best Regards,
Sylvain Pasdeloup
General Manager
Sofitel Bali Nusa Dua Beach Resort

reviews.title: Feels more like disney resort or casino hotel rather than resort in bali.
reviews.text: The loud noise from nikki beach club from morning to late night really bother the regular guest at sofitel. The pool is blocked by massive landscape so you cant feel the sunset from the pool, unlike most resort at nusadua. Room is fairly new and clean. Breakfast is awesome with vast selection but the crowd is really like you are in the casino and the fact that it is in the basement is kind of annoying. But the food there is nice,  also at the Cut Cucina Restaurant.

reviews.title: Expensive and over sized hotel ...better for families and groups.
reviews.text: If a resort hotel the size of a small town is your idea of a relaxing environment this Sofitel might be what you're looking for. The room was very clean and of a high standard. The service was excellent and the variety of food and restaurants quite mind blowing. The beach area is also immaculate with beautiful white sand and nice spacious sun beds. It's also quite busy with large events and over the weekend we stayed a large sound system was being installed for a wedding reception near the beach. Those responsible were conducting excruciatingly loud sound and mic checks utterly oblivious to the fact that paying guests were trying to vacation a few meters away. This lack of consideration towards guests is something that really undermines the whole experience of staying at such a large hotel. The aloofness of the staff in general is something many people may find acceptable but because I had stayed at a smaller hotel in Bali before relocating here, the lack of intimacy and atmosphere for couples was a negative.

reviews.title: Not sure if it is worth a 5-star
reviews.text: The check in was slow and the receptionist feels more like a sales person selling packages and stuff rather than letting us know if they are having a promo.. the check out was fast and efficient though.The bedroom and bathroom was clean and spacious. The bed was not as good and need to be replaced, it was not as firm and kind of depressed on both sides. The dinner buffet was not worth the money compared to the breakfast buffet. It was way more expensive with way less choices. The staff was all friendly and smiley but not fast or coordinated. We saw one westerner asking the concierge for band-aid and in the end after a long wait and rejections from the staff around the concierge (there are many of them just standing around doing nothing), she got none and had to leave....

And finally very long line during check out. No one is available to process my check out. reviews.room_type: LUXURY ROOM, 1 King Size Bed, Plunge Pool

reviews.title: Musty smell
reviews.text:
Stayed 4 days but the first two days were horrible. The very first room was musty and had mold on the side lamps  and also the shower head was kinda faulty. I decided to call the staff only the very next day as I thought I couldn’t stand the smell anymore. They brought me to another room and ask me whether is it still the same but all I can smell is the air freshener. The same smell occur when I came back in the late afternoon even with the dehumidifier on. By the second night I developed a headache as the musty smell is so strong coming from the sheets and interior. I couldn’t take it anymore and called for the duty manager and he did his best and reassured me that the 3rd and 4th day will be fine as I was about to book another hotel because I don’t want to look like some fussy customer. Indeed Ricky the duty manager put me in a room where is clean and of course the room doesn’t smell anything like the previous two. He has the best customer service. We are glad that the last two nights were perfect. 

Breakfast wise was kinda boring and the selection was not great and the pasty tasted not nice?  The neighbour Mulia hotel had the best breakfast I would say.
reviews.response:
Bonjour, Teo!

Thank you for taking the time to share your detailed feedback on your stay at Sofitel Bali Nusa Dua Beach Resort. We appreciate your patience and the opportunity to address your concerns during your visit.

It is great to hear that Ricky, our duty manager, was able to assist and ensure that your last two nights were comfortable and enjoyable. Providing a clean and pleasant environment for our guests is a priority, however, I am saddened to know that it was not up to your expectations.

We also acknowledge your feedback regarding the breakfast selection and appreciate your input. We will ensure to pass along your comments to our relevant department for further review and action. 

We value your time with us and hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back for an even better experience in the future.

Anne

reviews.title: Good, but  overpriced
reviews.text:
We stayed at Luxury Twin Plunge Pool room. The room had very strong smell of mould for the first day, almost impossible to normally breath. Either rat or squirrel was making noise at night inside aircon system. It was running and screaming inside the ventilation pipes. Service was very good though. All personnel is very friendly and helpful.
Spa and massage -  the massage was good, but twice as expensive as in Kempinski or Mulia or Ayana hotels (compared to a regular relaxation massage 90 min).

reviews.title: Nice Location
reviews.text:
Very good property . Friendly well spoken staff .
Lacking entertainment which we noticed other resorts had . Not much to do in the area at night so some form of entertainment would of been good. Have to book every night to eat in house otherwise you will be seated after 8 pm. Food was ok nothing to rave about though. Overall 3.5 stars out of 5.

reviews.room_type: Luxury King Pool Access Club Millesime

reviews.title: Food is Horrible/ Kids Club No good
reviews.text:
All the Food in the buffet was cold, and had no flavour. Alacarte menu needs to be redone as shockingly bad for what's suppose to be a 5 star resort. Food services need a major overall. Some of the worst food we've ever had.

Our daughter attended the Easter event  at the kids club and it was very disorganized and disappointing. while all the staff were outside painting the kids faces the rest of the children were running around inside unsupervised throwing ice and party food that had been set out, no activities other than face painting were planned and my daughter sad bored for over an hour waiting for the Easter egg hunt to find that there were no chocolate eggs instead hard boiled eggs were hidden. Very disappointing- what kid wants a hard boiled egg. I am glad I stayed with her to witness this, and would not recommend sending your kids to the kids club as staff don't seem to know what they are doing and were unable to manage the kids. 
Don't waste your money at this resort there are far better places in Nusa Dua with great activities for the kids and amazing food! Hilton and Melia are top notch and 10 times better!

reviews.title: A little overrated
reviews.text:
We read all the reviews and came under the assumption this resort would be fabulous! And not denying it was beautiful but a little overrated. 
1. Check in - we were told to wait in the lobby and when our bags were up in our room we would be escorted and shown to our room. They forgot us, 45 mins later I had to go and ask what was going on? To which they quickly took us up to our room (no tour) 
2. Pool was nice but nothing to get excited over. This place has so much potential as it’s on the beach and they’ve missed the mark. 
3. Buffet - flies all over the food 
4. There was an event during our stay (couldn’t be helped we are ok with that) but the loud music and noise all day and night. I felt so sorry for the people who had booked a beach view room, all they had were drunk people/people on a Conference and loud music till past midnight.
5. We walked down the road in Nusa dua and found much nicer restaurants and more well priced - found the prices very high for what they served.

Over all was nice and relaxing stay, but we probably will not return next time in Bali.

reviews.title: Experience at Sofitel
reviews.text:
Always wanted to visit the Sofitel and finally did on July 2022.

Pros;
- Nice resort with facilities. Beach club adds to the experience for the middle, younger aged individuals, along with the pool and pool bar - would be nicer if there was a deeper section. 
- Comfortable rooms and easy to travel along the beach to visit other resorts.
- Staff are friendly and helpful but still being trained.

Cons;
- Pricey resort, definitely not for the budget travellor. This is the same for the other resorts nearby, definitely shop around as this resorts price can add up quite quickly.
- More information about the restaurants and reservations upon checkin. As the restaurants fill up quite fast, if you don't have a reservation you will either need to wait or find somewhere else to eat. This would have been valuable information upon checkin. 

Besides that nice resort on the beach and got to go for walks on the walking trail between resorts.

reviews.title: Everything was good except the Kids Villa
reviews.text: The kids villa was below our expectation. It was so hot inside. I didn't know how many air cons were on but indoor area was very hot. The toys were not so many and most of them are broken. They do not let the children under 5 yo without parents or nannys to play along in the room. However, if you ask them to supervise they will charge you for IDR 150.000 per child. If you have 2 kids like mine, you need to supervise both of them by yourself too. My younger child was stuck in one of the car and the staff there, they really didn't help him to get out from it. So, you do really need supervise your children by yourself. They limit the maximum time to play was 2 hours for 1 entry. So you cannot play 1 hour in the morning and anothet 1 hour in the afternoon.  It's funny they asked everyone in the room to use socks, but they let the housekeeper went into the room without it. I saw the house keeper cleaning the glass window for quite long with barefoot and walking around the playroom.

reviews.title: Good but not great
reviews.text: The brand is renowned for its luxury tag however the following made us question their reputation. To start with a positive, the staff were excellent, their Xmas spirit was amazing  and the location great however I believe there needs to be more attention to the following... The housekeeping compared to other 5 star hotels we have stayed in was below average. When we arrived our bar fridge was empty, they arrived on day 4 to fill it. There were minimal items like tissues and toilet paper so we found ourselves continually calling asking for more. One day we found ourselves with only one towel left in the room! We had a plunge pool room which was ok. The outlook was onto a car park. The pool was slimy when we arrived and my wife refused to swim in it however it was cleaned and chlorinated the next day. The surrounding gardens were full of weeds, the ground was also muddy in spots. There was also moss on some of the steps and walls which needed cleaning. Overall the hotel rooms seem that they are starting to become tired. The breakfast wasn't too good, not a good choice of western based food and what was there wasn't cooked too well. One person making coffees for what seemed up to 200 guests over breakfast and NEVER ever bought teaspoons. We had to ask every single day. The restaurant was always hot as they had no internal air con so sitting inside meant you had to fight to sit near a portable air con and battle flies etc from outside as the doors were left open for air - and the flies were plentiful. Overall we didn't hate our stay, we got by but concluded on these experiences we would not stay there again. The brand is recognised as a luxury chain so we were expecting a certain level but these issues make it hard to rate it favourably.

reviews.title: Great hotel but avoid the cocktails.
reviews.text: Everything was great at this resort except for the cocktails at happy hour by the pool. I ordered a pineapple based cocktail and for some reason it tasted like toothpastes and maybe detergent. I say maybe detergent as I've never tasted detergent so don't actually know what it taste like but it had some weird chemical taste and it I s the only thing I can think of that was behind the bar that was a chemical. As it was happy hour I received two cocktails at the same time (I requested this) the second drink tasted as it should. I inquired on the ingredients to make the cocktail and there was no mint/spearmint/peppermit to speak of so asked the bar staff why I have a drink that tasted like toothpaste and chemical? He just smiled and said sorry I'm not sure why it has a mint/chemical flovour. He did offered to make me another cocktail which I declined at first but then changed my mind and said yes and watched him make it this time. I gave both the cocktails to my friends who were happy to have them and I just ordered a Bintang as I wasn't really in the mood for a cocktail anymore. I found this whole situation very strange and I also found the bar staff at L'Oh bar not as professional and efficient as the rest of the hotel staff. At one point there was at least five staff on and not one customer at the bar but they all were talking and laughing and I had to walk around the bar and wave to get their attention. Overall I had a great stay here but not sure if I'll come back as I got sick the next day and I really believe it was the cocktail. This was my second visit to this bar and I must say the first lot of staff here were great and so were the cocktails.

And what surprised me was when I entered the room, I almost fell at the shower area, because the floor was all wet (it's just like previous guest had shower before and the house keeping forgot to clean it), and one of the bin was not cleaned, so it still had lots of garbage in it..  And the hairdryer is not working properly as well, so they changed it for me. Very surprised with all of it, because for class of hotel like Sofitel, you expect more. But the good thing is, after I complaint and told them about it, they clean my room very detail since. The staff are very nice, the facilities and environment of the hotel also awesome.. But might not coming back here, and will try other hotel with the same level like Sofitel.

reviews.title: Just OK
reviews.text: I got a good deal on Sofitel for two nights. But my overall experience was just OK. For a 5 star resort I felt that they indeed ticked all the standard requirements, but I never felt that 5 star service overall. Their housekeeping was a slow to respond and we tend to had to call twice to guest services or housekeeping to ensure that our request were attended to. One of the first was that they had a broken scale in the room and we literally had to call more than once to ensure that they changed it. Also they didn't provide any masks or sanitizer. I know COVID has subdued, but this should be standard. We also asked, but this wasn't attended to.  Food was also just OK. The only good thing was the breakfast selections, though it was surprising that they did not serve standard selections such as croissants and even fried noodles on the day we had braeakfast (which is an Indonesian staple). This was on the 10th of May 2022.  Going back to the deal I got for staying two nights here, I couldn't complain much, but again it diluted the overal 5 star experience that one should have when staying at Sofitel.

Surprisingly, there was a halal section at the buffet breakfast. The pool is huge and they have a kids villa with activities for kids. The beach is private and clean unlike those you get in Kuta or Seminyak area. We also dined at a halal restaurant nearby - Tamarind Mediterranean Brassiere. There’s a free shuttle bus to Bali Collection. Overall, it was an enjoyable stay and the service was impeccable!

reviews.text:
We were pleasantly surprised by the room we were given upon arrival. However that changed the first night by the awful karaoke event that the Club Med subjected us to on our first night. So we moved to the other side of the hotel where the two rooms we looked at although the same layout were distinctly musky in smell. Quality sleep always prevails!
Breakfast is always a busy affair, selection is vast and tasty. Club Milliseme is worthwhile, its just a shame where it is located in the hotel. It would have been great if there was more outdoor seating that was better lit. However breakfast there is not great, very inconsistent and delayed.
They are still within their soft opening stage, however as a group you do expect a higher standard of attention to detail as a number of staff have vast international experience(utilise their knowledge!)
Shame that some if the communal areas are shabby, housekeeping need to step it up dusty mirrors in lifts, dirty marks on walls and stairwells. The toilet at the pool area is disgusting! Even on my first visit to use with a member of staff there, no impetus to tidy or make clean. Never any toilet paper or paper towels, to use. Also handwash was no existent.Appalling ! Would be back interesting to see how was they would cope at high occupancy, especially when placing orders for food/drinks at the beach bar.

reviews.title: Tres Magnifique Service
reviews.text: Staff very attentive and friendly. Highly Impressed by their communications skills, which helps foster a friendship level and caring attitude. We got spoiled fr F&B dept! Overall, KUDOS to those who served us at Kwee Zeen, Pool Bar, and the Italian Restaurant. Hats off to the Management who provide excellent training to the staff. Best ever service from staff we have ever encountered in the whole world!(have travelled extensively). Beach kept cleaned; deck chairs in order. Pool towers neatly rolled on each deck chair. A pool attendant will come by to set and place the towel on the deck chair of your choice. Enjoyed the personal touch!Unfortunately, there are two set backs. An Indian Wedding function held for the 3 days we were there. All 300 or so. Loud music. Letter came apologizing certain part of the pool and a restaurant outlet will be closed for that private function. What a let down! Would hv appreciated a "warning" or an "alert" of such event is to happen.  Cd have come on another date. But No! Lobby packed with wedding entourage, bfast time was filled and noisy, long queue for room check-in. Had Int'l Buffet Dinner to end our stay. To our disappointment, poor quality of food spread compared to the b'fast varieties. There was a good spill over of Indian dishes that came from the wedding. Limited choices! Amazingly, something standard like soup cannot be found in the buffet. Sadly food quality had dampened our impression of this great hotel. Providing good info to all guests are crucial to avoid disappointment. Unfair for us to have spent so much to fly into Bali to face this in the hotel you've chosen. Will we come back again? Answer is YES. But will call first if there is another wedding or a huge conference taking place.
reviews.positives: Friendliness of Staff; Beautiful & Comfortable Room; Best Buffet Breakfast spread!

reviews.title: Clearly not a place for Beach
reviews.text:
I will start with the negative sides as most of the people would be seeking those information:

- Overall Bali is not a great place for Beach. The Sofitel Nosa Dua is the perfect example of a beach you can find on the island: poor colors of the water, low visibility and high tides. We even encountered some jelly fishes.
- The Sofitel lacks of Sport activities. Only a fitness room is proposed. There are few water activities proposed on the beach but you will have to walk a bit to find them (do not hesitate to bargain the price)

On the bright side:
- Amazing Bedding, I haven't slept so well for ages !
- Very good quality of the staff
- Great Swimming pool and spa

I definitely suggest the Sofitel for a stay but i will probably not go back to Bali as there are better places in Asia to see.
reviews.positives: Spa, Service, Bedding

reviews.title: Wonderful stay
reviews.text:
We had a wonderful stay, the overall hotel, interior, swimming pool, beach and our room with plunge pool were more than we expected, as well as the food selection and quality, all was amazing.
The staff were extremely polite and very helpful at all times and overall service was excellent. 
The only few remarks I would mention is as the hotel occupancy increased, it was difficult to order on the beach (sun-beds) as the service was mainly focused on the beach bar/restaurant. Many times we had to get up and walk to the restaurant to place our order. On another occasion they ran out of beach towels and the last two we got were ripped with large holes in them, which wasn’t pleasant at all.
More significant remark however was the treatment from the hostess or waitress in Cucina on our first day as were rejected to have a dinner at 5.30 (we were willing to wait until 6 when the restaurant opens) with explanation that they are fully booked and cannot accommodate 2 persons, although we explained that we just arrived and were not informed about a mandatory reservation and were given a suggestion to come back at 9.30 that evening and no other alternative solution, for ex. a limited seating time.
As we passed through the restaurant area over the  next few hours we could only see 3-4 tables occupied. Hence, we found it quite unfair for in-house guests following an evening dress code on a first night of their honeymoon to be rejected for a simple dinner. 

Despite everything, we had a truly amazing time and definitely the professionalism of the staff and atmosphere of the hotel, for us wins over the few slip-ups.

reviews.title: Not for Couples
reviews.text: So I should start by saying that I never write reviews, especially bad ones.  However, I read at least 1000 reviews before booking where we would honeymoon and I would like to help anyone else looking for the same reason.  The facilities and staff at this hotel are nothing short of amazing, the rooms are fantastic and the food is delicious.  Here comes the but... Our entire honeymoon was overshadowed by poorly behaved children EVERYWHERE.  When we went to breakfast and it was complete mayhem, just tons of families and babies screaming we expected as such from a large resort where you can opt to buy breakfast.  We did not expect it at the beach, at the bars, at the "intimate restaurants".  We went to the swim up bar just to have some alone time and each time we went to the bar area there were small children unattended screaming and playing.  Listen, I try to coexist, but there were 1000 kids activities and children designated areas.
reviews.response:
Dear Shelby,

Bonjour! Selamat Siang!

We very much appreciate the time you spent for reviewing us online on Agoda.

It delights us to have you celebrated your honeymoon with us. However, knowing that it did not happened as how you have expected it to be truly saddened us. Please rest assured that your feedback has been shared with the relevant department for immediate measures.

Again, we look forward to welcoming you back in the near future.


Kind Regard,
Sylvain Pasdeloup
General Manager

reviews.title: Great stay, dissappointing breakfast
reviews.text:
Came here on one weekend for a staycation, just because. Impulsively booked the room just few days earlier and didn't check properly. Turns out I booked regular room without breakfast. Upon check-in the staff offered and upgrade for Pool-Access room which very near the main pool, including breakfast and executive lounge access. 1,2 M IDR and I think it's not a bad deal. 
First day was great, afternoon tea and evening cocktal (include light dinner) was great. I enjoyed the massive pool also. All the staff are friendly and readily available without being intrusive, which is a great balance. 
The dissappointment for me is the fact that Cucina is closed for a private event. During the saturday afternoon, the pool area is very noisy with Indian music as they are having a pool party. It even gets very noisy in my room, as it's pretty close to the pool. 
Another big dissappointment is breakfast at Kweezeen. This is so bad, I can't hardly believe its 5* resort.

We called in the maintenance they brought in the engineers which took forever to come, as a result we were not in the room when they came, but they did come. They came twice on the first day and finally we had to sleep in another room for the first night. Afterwards we just decided to suck it up because they have no other rooms available anyway. Huge disappointment.

We had a truly memorable stay! We were pleasantly surprised to be upgraded to the Prestige Suite and selected as "Guests of the Day" — such a thoughtful touch. Lisa was very helpful and informative at the front desk. Waiting for us in the room was a lovely surprise: cake, fresh coconut water, fruit, and personalized welcome messages. The suite was incredibly spacious, and the highlight for me was the extra king-size bed — unbelievably comfortable!
The staff were exceptional — so attentive, warm, and welcoming throughout our stay. It’s those little gestures and genuine hospitality that made this experience stand out.
reviews.negatives: We were a bit disappointed with the breakfast, especially given the overall high standards of the hotel. While there appeared to be a wide variety of options, many of the dishes lacked flavor. The fruit in particular was surprisingly tasteless — quite unexpected, especially in a country known for its delicious, fresh produce available everywhere.

reviews.title: A complete heaven for the stomach
reviews.text:
Fantastic hotel with wonderful facilities and amenities. Plunge pool is terrific. Swimming pools are superb for all ages. Children and adults can swim together without being worried. Nice pool bar, great kids' pool. Food is EXQUISITE!! All types of food are being done nicely with the taste of heaven... especially in the bread department. A bit pricey... but phenomenal.
If only the attention to detail is being addressed more, it will be truely a 5 star hotel. The broken light of the "do not disturb/ please make up room" sign at our door is broken... and after reporting it 3 times, nothing is being done. Coffee sachets are not being refilled, dirty towels were not being replaced, the back door of our garden was left unlocked after being cleaned. We experienced, however, everyday improvements from the staff in trying to give us the best experience.... in which we truely appreciate. 
Thanm you Sofitel for making our stay memorable.
reviews.positives: Food, Room and swimming Pool

reviews.title: Perfect if you want to be away from the crowd
reviews.text: Resort located in Nusa Dua together with all 5 stars resorts. Room is very spacious, bathroom, toiletries, are all great. Staff is also friendly. Wouldn’t complain about it. There are couple of things that bothered me. First, TV channel is so basic, they don’t even have HBO, or smart TV. We stayed in the resort so we can just chill in the room or resort area most of the time but unfortunately the TV sucks and there are not much things to do in the area besides pool, a couple restaurants, and sunbathing. Second, up most, I hate that they’re not allowing gojeks to come down to the hotel area. NONE. I feel like it’s very rude. I ordered a pizza through go-food and he said the security is not allowing him to go through the entrance gate. That means, me, as a guest, I have to WALK all the way, not just to the lobby, but to the entrance gate of the resort, which is 5-10 minutes walking distance. I asked the security why he won’t allow gojeks to go through and he said he doesn’t want bikes to disrupt other guests coming in the lobby. It was at 9:30 PM at night.
reviews.response:

At Sofitel, we value our guest's feedback as we believe it could help us much to improve the quality of products and services in the future, hence, we would like to thank you once again for sharing it with us. Nonetheless, we hope this did not fluster your experience much nor deter you from coming back again to our resort in the future.

reviews.title: Very good with a couple of caveats
reviews.text:
One needs to be careful when writing a review any resort or establishment in Bali keeping in mind the devastating impact of Covid on a destination primarily reliant on tourism.  Having said that, reviews need to be fair and transparent so we the traveling public can rely on each other when making holiday choices.

So, to the Sofitel Nusa Dua.  Overall very very good.  In summary on the positives:

Great location if quality beach front is important. 
Great beach chairs and huts to sit under.
Water is clear and clean (superficially anyway!).  Sand is nice.
Pool is large and very nice albeit pretty crowded in January.  But no complaints.
Room is big and spacious.  Nicely appointed with a lovely balcony with swinging and rocking chairs to sit on.
Bed was comfortable and large (see negatives below).
Breakfast was as good as can be expected from a Sofitel - all the usual offerings but if you compare it to other Sofitels that I have stayed in, it lacks the class and sophistication of the breakfasts I’ve had there (mainly the quality of things like the cheeses and meats - but I’m being a little picky).
Staff - always exceptional here.  Top class.

Negatives - things to watch out for:

I mentioned the beach above.  If you think you can have a relaxing time listening to the waves and enjoying tranquility forget it!!  Sofitel employ a band of 3 musicians who play loud (often rock) music for pretty much the best part of the day (11am - 3pm)  at the beachfront restaurant.  To be honest it’s a reason I would not return.  While Sofitel are accommodating the minority of guests who attend the beachfront restaurant, they completely forget about those of us who choose to use the beach front as a point of relaxation.  I strongly suggest if management read this review they think about a better way to provide entertainment that is conducive to the environment.  To me it was annoying to the point of being a consideration if I return to the hotel despite all the other positives.

The room.
